data.title: Survey Reveals Increased Cryptocurrency Interest Among Wealthy Americans
data.description: According to PANews, a recent survey conducted by Grayscale and Harris Poll indicates that over 26% of high-net-worth individuals in the United States, possessing investable assets of $1 million or more, currently hold cryptocurrencies. Furthermore, nearly 38% of these affluent investors anticipate incorporating cryptocurrencies into their portfolios in the future. The data suggests that since the approval of spot cryptocurrency ETPs early last year, high-net-worth investors have become more attentive to economic conditions and increasingly confident in cryptocurrency investments.

data.title: Bitcoin Volatility Reaches Highest Level Since Last September
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Bitcoin's volatility has continued to rise, reaching its highest point since September of last year, currently reported at 3.45%.

data.title: Whales Acquire 130 Million ADA in Recent Transactions
data.description: According to PANews, cryptocurrency analyst Ali Martinez revealed on the X platform that whales have purchased approximately 130 million ADA in the past 72 hours. This significant acquisition highlights the ongoing interest and activity in the ADA market.

data.title: Wormhole Announces Integration of Circle’s New Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol CCTP V2
data.description: Cross-chain interoperability protocol Wormhole announced on X Platform that it has integrated Circle’s new cross-chain transfer protocol CCTP V2 to support USDC transfers between Avalanche, Base, and Ethereum blockchains. Its Wormhole Settlement function will also provide expansion options for developers building multi-chain applications.

data.title: SEC’s “Make IPOs Great Again” Plan Sparks XRP Speculation Amid Crypto Reforms
data.description:
The U.S. SEC has hinted at a major regulatory shift under the new administration, as the Trump administration vows to revamp crypto policies.
source: https://zycrypto.com/secs-make-ipos-great-again-plan-sparks-xrp-speculation-amid-crypto-reforms/

data.title: U.S. Senate Committee Advances Stablecoin Regulation Bill
data.description:
According to PANews, the U.S. Senate Banking Committee has approved the GENIUS Act, a bill aimed at regulating stablecoin issuers. This legislation is designed to facilitate the use of stablecoins as a payment method beyond the cryptocurrency trading sector. However, the bill has raised concerns about the potential privatization of the U.S. dollar and an increased risk of de-dollarization.

The GENIUS Act permits stablecoin issuers to operate without the same federal consumer financial protection measures that apply to credit card and peer-to-peer payment platforms. Additionally, it allows these issuers to invest in money market funds, repurchase agreements, and uninsured bank deposits. These assets have previously received bailouts during financial crises, which could indirectly impact the U.S. dollar.

Data indicates that the proportion of the U.S. dollar in global foreign exchange reserves has decreased from over 70% at the beginning of the 21st century to below 60%.

data.title: Astar zkEVM Network Shutdown Approaches, Users Urged to Withdraw Assets
data.description: According to Odaily, Astar has announced on X that the shutdown date for the Astar zkEVM network is approaching. Users are advised to withdraw all assets by April 1. Previously, in January, Astar had announced the commencement of the migration from Astar zkEVM to Soneium.

data.title: A newly created address has accumulated 12.15 million SHELL through Binance in the past two days, worth nearly 3.5 million US dollars
data.description: Onchain Lens monitoring shows that a newly created address has withdrawn and hoarded 12.15 million SHELLs worth US$3.49 million through Binance in the past two days.
